  status,
  created_at
FROM contractor_details
GRANT SELECT ON safe_contractor_details TO authenticated
DROP VIEW IF EXISTS safe_machinery_details CASCADE

CREATE VIEW safe_machinery_details 
WITH (security_invoker = true)
AS
SELECT 
  id,
  profile_id,
  specialization,
  machinery_name,
  machinery_model,
  hourly_rate,
  daily_rate,
  is_available,
  created_at
FROM machinery_details
GRANT SELECT ON safe_machinery_details TO authenticated

-- Now we need to add a SELECT policy to profiles for authenticated users
-- to view public profile info (but NOT phone)
-- Since views with security_invoker will respect the underlying table's RLS,
-- we need an RLS policy that allows SELECT but the view already filters out phone

-- Add policy for authenticated users to view all profiles (safe data only via view)
-- This allows the safe_profiles view to work
CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can view profiles via safe view" 
ON public.profiles 
FOR SELECT 
TO authenticated
USING (true)

ON public.engineer_details 
FOR SELECT

USING (true)

-- CRITICAL FIX: The previous policy allowed all authenticated users to read profiles directly
-- This would expose phone numbers. We need a different approach.

-- STRATEGY: Use Column-Level Security via Views
-- 1. The safe_profiles view (security_invoker) already excludes phone
-- 2. But with USING(true), users can also query profiles directly and see phone
-- 3. We need to rely on the SECURE RPC functions instead

-- Remove the overly permissive policy we just created
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Authenticated users can view profiles via safe view" ON public.profiles
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Authenticated users can view engineer details via safe view" ON public.engineer_details
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Authenticated users can view craftsman details via safe view" ON public.craftsman_details
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Authenticated users can view contractor details via safe view" ON public.contractor_details
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Authenticated users can view machinery details via safe view" ON public.machinery_details

-- Drop the security_invoker views since they won't work without underlying RLS access
-- We'll rely on the existing secure RPC functions instead: get_public_profile, get_public_profiles, search_profiles_safe
DROP VIEW IF EXISTS safe_profiles CASCADE
DROP VIEW IF EXISTS safe_engineer_details CASCADE
DROP VIEW IF EXISTS safe_craftsman_details CASCADE
DROP VIEW IF EXISTS safe_contractor_details CASCADE

-- get_public_profile(uuid) - Returns profile without phone
-- get_public_profiles(uuid[]) - Returns profiles without phone  
-- search_profiles_safe(...) - Returns profiles without phone
-- get_public_profile_with_details(uuid) - Returns profile with specialization details without phone

-- These functions are SECURITY DEFINER with search_path = public, which is the correct approach.
-- No changes needed to the functions.

-- Ensure the profiles table RLS only allows owners to read their own data (phone included)
-- The existing policies already do this correctly:
-- "Users can view their own full profile" - USING (auth.uid() = user_id)
-- "Users can view their own profile" - USING (auth.uid() = user_id)

-- Add explicit deny policies for anonymous access to sensitive tables

-- 1. PROFILES TABLE - Deny anonymous access
-- Create a restrictive policy that explicitly requires authentication
CREATE POLICY "Deny anonymous access to profiles" 
ON public.profiles 
FOR SELECT 
TO anon
USING (false)

-- Also fix INSERT policy to require auth
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Users can insert own profile" ON public.profiles

CREATE POLICY "Users can insert own profile" 
ON public.profiles 
FOR INSERT 
TO authenticated
WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id)

-- Fix UPDATE policy to use authenticated role
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Users can update own profile" ON public.profiles

CREATE POLICY "Users can update own profile" 
ON public.profiles 
FOR UPDATE 
TO authenticated
USING (auth.uid() = user_id)
WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id)

-- 2. ADMIN_USERS TABLE - Deny all non-admin access explicitly
-- Add explicit deny for anon
CREATE POLICY "Deny anonymous access to admin_users" 
ON public.admin_users 
FOR ALL 
TO anon
USING (false)
WITH CHECK (false)

-- Update existing SELECT policies to use authenticated role and is_admin check
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Admins can view admin users" ON public.admin_users
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Block non-admin access to admin_users" ON public.admin_users

CREATE POLICY "Admins can view admin users" 
ON public.admin_users 
FOR SELECT 
TO authenticated
USING (is_admin())

-- Add a restrictive policy for authenticated non-admins
CREATE POLICY "Non-admins cannot access admin_users" 
ON public.admin_users 
FOR SELECT 
TO authenticated
USING (is_admin())

-- Update other admin_users policies to use authenticated role
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Super admins can delete admin users" ON public.admin_users

CREATE POLICY "Super admins can delete admin users" 
ON public.admin_users 
FOR DELETE 
TO authenticated
USING (is_super_admin())
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Super admins can insert admin users" ON public.admin_users

CREATE POLICY "Super admins can insert admin users" 
ON public.admin_users 
FOR INSERT 
TO authenticated
WITH CHECK (is_super_admin())
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Super admins can update admin users" ON public.admin_users

CREATE POLICY "Super admins can update admin users" 
ON public.admin_users 
FOR UPDATE 
TO authenticated
USING (is_super_admin() OR (id = get_admin_user_id()))
WITH CHECK (is_super_admin() OR (id = get_admin_user_id()))

-- =====================================================

-- Avatars policies

-- Stories policies

    AND auth.uid() IS NOT NULL
  )

-- 8. Create storage usage tracking table
-- =====================================================
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.storage_usage (
  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  profile_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES profiles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  bucket_name text NOT NULL,
  file_path text NOT NULL,
  file_size bigint NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  mime_type text,
  created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  UNIQUE(profile_id, bucket_name, file_path)
)

-- Enable RLS
ALTER TABLE public.storage_usage ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY

-- Users can view their own usage
CREATE POLICY "users_view_own_usage" ON public.storage_usage
  FOR SELECT USING (profile_id IN (SELECT id FROM profiles WHERE user_id = auth.uid()))

-- Users can insert their own usage records
CREATE POLICY "users_insert_own_usage" ON public.storage_usage
  FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (profile_id IN (SELECT id FROM profiles WHERE user_id = auth.uid()))

-- Users can delete their own usage records
CREATE POLICY "users_delete_own_usage" ON public.storage_usage
  FOR DELETE USING (profile_id IN (SELECT id FROM profiles WHERE user_id = auth.uid()))

-- Create indexes for fast lookups
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_storage_usage_profile ON public.storage_usage(profile_id)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_storage_usage_bucket ON public.storage_usage(bucket_name)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_storage_usage_created ON public.storage_usage(created_at DESC)

-- 9. Function to get user storage stats
-- =====================================================
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_user_storage_stats(p_profile_id uuid)
RETURNS TABLE (
  bucket_name text,
  file_count bigint,
  total_size bigint,
  formatted_size text
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
STABLE SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path TO 'public'
AS $$
BEGIN
  RETURN QUERY
  SELECT 
    su.bucket_name,
    COUNT(*)::bigint as file_count,
    COALESCE(SUM(su.file_size), 0)::bigint as total_size,
    CASE 
      WHEN COALESCE(SUM(su.file_size), 0) >= 1073741824 THEN 
        ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(su.file_size), 0)::numeric / 1073741824, 2)::text || ' GB'
      WHEN COALESCE(SUM(su.file_size), 0) >= 1048576 THEN 
        ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(su.file_size), 0)::numeric / 1048576, 2)::text || ' MB'
      WHEN COALESCE(SUM(su.file_size), 0) >= 1024 THEN 
        ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(su.file_size), 0)::numeric / 1024, 2)::text || ' KB'
      ELSE 
        COALESCE(SUM(su.file_size), 0)::text || ' B'
    END as formatted_size
  FROM storage_usage su
  WHERE su.profile_id = p_profile_id
  GROUP BY su.bucket_name
  ORDER BY total_size DESC;
END;
$$

-- 10. Function to cleanup orphaned files (admin only)
-- =====================================================
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_storage_overview()
RETURNS TABLE (
  bucket_name text,
  total_files bigint,
  total_size bigint,
  formatted_size text,
  unique_users bigint
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
STABLE SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path TO 'public'
AS $$
BEGIN
  -- Only allow admins
  IF NOT public.is_admin() THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Unauthorized';
  END IF;

  RETURN QUERY
  SELECT 
    su.bucket_name,
    COUNT(*)::bigint as total_files,
    COALESCE(SUM(su.file_size), 0)::bigint as total_size,
    CASE 
      WHEN COALESCE(SUM(su.file_size), 0) >= 1073741824 THEN 
        ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(su.file_size), 0)::numeric / 1073741824, 2)::text || ' GB'
      WHEN COALESCE(SUM(su.file_size), 0) >= 1048576 THEN 
        ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(su.file_size), 0)::numeric / 1048576, 2)::text || ' MB'
      ELSE 
        ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(su.file_size), 0)::numeric / 1024, 2)::text || ' KB'
    END as formatted_size,
    COUNT(DISTINCT su.profile_id)::bigint as unique_users
  FROM storage_usage su
  GROUP BY su.bucket_name
  ORDER BY total_size DESC;
END;
$$

-- Drop and recreate search_profiles_safe to not return user_id (sensitive field)
DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS public.search_profiles_safe(text, user_role, governorate, integer, integer)

CREATE FUNCTION public.search_profiles_safe(
  p_search_term text DEFAULT NULL,
  p_role user_role DEFAULT NULL,
  p_governorate governorate DEFAULT NULL,
  p_limit integer DEFAULT 20,
  p_offset integer DEFAULT 0
)
RETURNS TABLE(
  id uuid,
  full_name text,
  username text,
  avatar_url text,
  bio text,
  role user_role,
  governorate governorate,
  experience_years integer,
  rating numeric,
  total_reviews integer,
  is_verified boolean,
  followers_count integer,
  following_count integer
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
STABLE SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = 'public'
AS $$
DECLARE
  search_pattern text;
BEGIN
  -- Escape special LIKE characters to prevent injection
  IF p_search_term IS NOT NULL AND length(trim(p_search_term)) > 0 THEN
    -- Escape backslash first, then %, then _
    search_pattern := '%' || replace(replace(replace(trim(p_search_term), '\', '\\'), '%', '\%'), '_', '\_') || '%';
  END IF;
  
  -- Limit search term length to prevent DoS
  IF p_search_term IS NOT NULL AND length(p_search_term) > 100 THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Search term too long';
  END IF;
  
  -- Limit maximum results to prevent excessive data retrieval
  IF p_limit > 100 THEN
    p_limit := 100;
  END IF;
  
  RETURN QUERY
  SELECT 
    p.id,
    p.full_name,
    p.username,
    p.avatar_url,
    p.bio,
    p.role,
    p.governorate,
    p.experience_years,
    p.rating,
    p.total_reviews,
    p.is_verified,
    p.followers_count,
    p.following_count
  FROM profiles p
  WHERE 
    (search_pattern IS NULL OR p.full_name ILIKE search_pattern OR p.username ILIKE search_pattern)
    AND (p_role IS NULL OR p.role = p_role)
    AND (p_governorate IS NULL OR p.governorate = p_governorate)
  ORDER BY p.rating DESC NULLS LAST, p.created_at DESC
  LIMIT p_limit
  OFFSET p_offset;
END;
$$

-- Fix chat-media storage bucket to restrict access to conversation participants only
-- This prevents public access to private conversation media

-- First, drop the existing permissive public read policy
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "chat_media_public_read" ON storage.objects
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Allow public read access" ON storage.objects
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "chat_media_select" ON storage.objects

-- Create a more restrictive policy that only allows conversation participants to view chat media
-- The media file path should contain the conversation_id for proper authorization
CREATE POLICY "chat_media_participant_read" ON storage.objects
  FOR SELECT USING (
    bucket_id = 'chat-media'
    AND auth.uid() IS NOT NULL
    AND (
      -- Allow if the user uploaded the file (owner path pattern: user_id/filename)
      (storage.foldername(name))[1] = auth.uid()::text
      OR
      -- Allow if user is a participant in any conversation that references this media
      EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM public.conversations c
        JOIN public.profiles p ON p.user_id = auth.uid()
        JOIN public.messages m ON m.conversation_id = c.id
        WHERE (c.participant_one = p.id OR c.participant_two = p.id)
        AND (
          m.image_url LIKE '%' || name || '%'
          OR m.audio_url LIKE '%' || name || '%'
        )
      )
    )
  )

AFTER INSERT OR DELETE ON public.post_comments

-- Batch conversation loading function (eliminates N+1 in MessagesScreen)
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_user_conversations(p_profile_id uuid)
RETURNS TABLE(
  conversation_id uuid,
  recipient_id uuid,
  recipient_name text,
  recipient_avatar text,
  last_message text,
  last_message_at timestamptz,
  unread_count bigint,
  is_muted boolean
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
STABLE SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path TO 'public'
AS $$
BEGIN
  RETURN QUERY
  SELECT 
    c.id as conversation_id,
    CASE WHEN c.participant_one = p_profile_id THEN c.participant_two ELSE c.participant_one END as recipient_id,
    rp.full_name as recipient_name,
    rp.avatar_url as recipient_avatar,
    c.last_message,
    c.last_message_at,
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM messages m WHERE m.conversation_id = c.id AND m.sender_id != p_profile_id AND m.is_read = false)::bigint as unread_count,
    EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM muted_conversations mc WHERE mc.conversation_id = c.id AND mc.profile_id = p_profile_id) as is_muted
  FROM conversations c
  JOIN profiles rp ON rp.id = (CASE WHEN c.participant_one = p_profile_id THEN c.participant_two ELSE c.participant_one END)
  LEFT JOIN blocked_users bu ON (bu.blocker_id = p_profile_id AND bu.blocked_id = rp.id)
  WHERE (c.participant_one = p_profile_id OR c.participant_two = p_profile_id)
    AND c.last_message IS NOT NULL
    AND bu.id IS NULL
  ORDER BY c.last_message_at DESC NULLS LAST;
END;
$$

-- Performance indexes for high-traffic operations
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_posts_active_created ON public.posts(is_active, created_at DESC) WHERE is_active = true

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_conv_unread ON public.messages(conversation_id, is_read) WHERE is_read = false
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_conversations_participants ON public.conversations(participant_one, participant_two, last_message_at DESC)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stories_profile_expires ON public.stories(profile_id, expires_at DESC)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_profile_unread ON public.notifications(profile_id, is_read) WHERE is_read = false
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_post_likes_post ON public.post_likes(post_id)

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_reel_likes_reel ON public.reel_likes(reel_id)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_reel_comments_reel ON public.reel_comments(reel_id)

-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
-- COMPREHENSIVE PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION MIGRATION
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

-- 1. STORIES - faster home feed loading
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stories_active_feed ON stories (profile_id, expires_at DESC) WHERE is_archived = false
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stories_expires ON stories (expires_at) WHERE is_archived = false

-- 2. CONVERSATIONS - faster message list
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_conversations_participant_one_last ON conversations (participant_one, last_message_at DESC)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_conversations_participant_two_last ON conversations (participant_two, last_message_at DESC)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_conversations_last_message_at ON conversations (last_message_at DESC NULLS LAST)

-- 3. MESSAGES - faster chat loading
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_conversation_created ON messages (conversation_id, created_at DESC)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_unread ON messages (conversation_id, sender_id, is_read) WHERE is_read = false

-- 4. NOTIFICATIONS - faster notification feed
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_profile_created ON notifications (profile_id, created_at DESC)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_unread ON notifications (profile_id, is_read) WHERE is_read = false

-- 5. AI MESSAGES - faster chat history
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ai_messages_conversation ON ai_messages (conversation_id, created_at ASC)

-- 6. AI CONVERSATIONS - faster list
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ai_conversations_profile ON ai_conversations (profile_id, updated_at DESC)

-- 7. REELS - faster feed
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_reels_active_feed ON reels (created_at DESC) WHERE is_active = true
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_reels_profile ON reels (profile_id, created_at DESC)

-- 8. REEL LIKES - faster like checks
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_reel_likes_reel ON reel_likes (reel_id, profile_id)

-- 9. REEL COMMENTS - faster comment loading
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_reel_comments_reel ON reel_comments (reel_id, created_at DESC)

-- 10. POST LIKES - faster like checks
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_post_likes_composite ON post_likes (post_id, profile_id)

-- 11. POST COMMENTS - faster loading
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_post_comments_post ON post_comments (post_id, created_at DESC)

-- 12. FOLLOWERS - faster lookups
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_followers_following ON followers (following_id, created_at DESC)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_followers_follower ON followers (follower_id, created_at DESC)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_followers_pair ON followers (follower_id, following_id)

-- 13. REVIEWS - faster profile reviews
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_reviews_reviewed ON reviews (reviewed_id, created_at DESC)

-- 14. PROJECTS - faster project listing
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_projects_profile ON projects (profile_id, created_at DESC)

-- 15. BLOCKED USERS - faster block checks
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_blocked_users_pair ON blocked_users (blocker_id, blocked_id)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_blocked_users_reverse ON blocked_users (blocked_id, blocker_id)

-- 16. MUTED CONVERSATIONS - faster mute checks
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_muted_conversations_pair ON muted_conversations (profile_id, conversation_id)

-- 17. STORY VIEWS/LIKES
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_story_views_story ON story_views (story_id, viewer_profile_id)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_story_likes_story ON story_likes (story_id, profile_id)

-- 18. SAVED REELS
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_saved_reels_profile ON saved_reels (profile_id, created_at DESC)

-- 19. SUBSCRIPTIONS - faster status checks
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_subscriptions_active ON subscriptions (profile_id, status) WHERE status = 'active'

-- 20. PROFILES - faster search
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_profiles_role_rating ON profiles (role, rating DESC NULLS LAST)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_profiles_user_id ON profiles (user_id)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_profiles_governorate ON profiles (governorate)

-- 21. Optimized home feed RPC - get posts with profile data in one query
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_home_feed(
  p_profile_id uuid,
  p_limit integer DEFAULT 20,
  p_offset integer DEFAULT 0
)
RETURNS TABLE(
  post_id uuid,
  content text,
  image_url text,
  post_type text,
  likes_count integer,
  comments_count integer,
  created_at timestamptz,
  profile_id uuid,
  full_name text,
  username text,
  avatar_url text,
  role public.user_role,
  is_verified boolean,
  is_liked boolean
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path TO 'public'
AS $function$
BEGIN
  RETURN QUERY
  SELECT 
    p.id as post_id,
    p.content,
    p.image_url,
    p.post_type,
    p.likes_count,
    p.comments_count,
    p.created_at,
    pr.id as profile_id,
    pr.full_name,
    pr.username,
    pr.avatar_url,
    pr.role,
    pr.is_verified,
    EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM post_likes pl WHERE pl.post_id = p.id AND pl.profile_id = p_profile_id) as is_liked
  FROM posts p
  JOIN profiles pr ON pr.id = p.profile_id
  LEFT JOIN blocked_users bu ON (bu.blocker_id = p_profile_id AND bu.blocked_id = p.profile_id)
    OR (bu.blocker_id = p.profile_id AND bu.blocked_id = p_profile_id)
  WHERE p.is_active = true 
    AND (p.is_archived = false OR p.is_archived IS NULL)
    AND bu.id IS NULL
  ORDER BY p.created_at DESC
  LIMIT p_limit
  OFFSET p_offset;
END;
$function$

-- 22. Optimized messages count RPC
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_unread_messages_count(p_profile_id uuid)
RETURNS bigint
LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path TO 'public'
AS $function$
  SELECT COALESCE(SUM(sub.cnt), 0)::bigint
  FROM (
    SELECT COUNT(*) as cnt
    FROM messages m
    JOIN conversations c ON c.id = m.conversation_id
    WHERE m.is_read = false
      AND m.sender_id != p_profile_id
      AND (c.participant_one = p_profile_id OR c.participant_two = p_profile_id)
  ) sub;
$function$

-- 23. Optimized batch mark messages as read
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.mark_messages_read(p_conversation_id uuid, p_reader_profile_id uuid)
RETURNS void
LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path TO 'public'
AS $function$
BEGIN
  UPDATE messages 
  SET is_read = true 
  WHERE conversation_id = p_conversation_id 
    AND sender_id != p_reader_profile_id 
    AND is_read = false;
END;
$function$

-- Insert the Engee system prompt
INSERT INTO public.app_config (key, value, description)
VALUES (
  'engee_system_prompt',
  'أنتِ "المهندسة الذكية انجي ENGEE" مساعدة ذكية متخصصة في تطبيق "مهندس" للربط بين المهندسين والمقاولين والحرفيين والزبائن.

معلومات عن التطبيق:
- تطبيق مهندس هو منصة تجمع بين المهندسين والمقاولين والمعماريين والحرفيين والزبائن
- يمكن للزبائن نشر مشاريع والبحث عن محترفين
- يمكن للمحترفين تصفح المشاريع وتقديم عروض
- التطبيق يدعم المحادثات المباشرة بين المستخدمين
- يوجد نظام تقييم ومراجعات للمحترفين
- يدعم التطبيق اللغة العربية والإنجليزية

أنتِ المهندسة الذكية انجي ENGEE، مهندسة استشارية محترفة وودودة، تجيبين على أسئلة المستخدمين بلطف واحترافية.
- إذا سأل المستخدم عن كيفية استخدام التطبيق، قدمي شرحاً واضحاً ومختصراً

DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS on_profile_created_subscription ON public.profiles

CREATE TRIGGER on_profile_created_subscription
AFTER INSERT ON public.profiles
FOR EACH ROW
EXECUTE FUNCTION public.handle_new_profile_subscription()

-- Update existing profiles that don't have a subscription
INSERT INTO public.subscriptions (profile_id, status, plan_type, starts_at, expires_at)
SELECT id, 'active', 'free', NOW(), NOW() + INTERVAL '10 years'
FROM public.profiles p
WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.subscriptions s WHERE s.profile_id = p.id)
ON CONFLICT (profile_id) DO NOTHING
